title: Can someone tell me what Im looking at here
display_title: Can someone tell me what Im looking at here
wikitext:
[]: Can someone tell me what I'm looking at here?             Quote Post by Clover » Thu Jun 03, 2010 8:52 pm -

[]: There, I have contributed to the community.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Sat Jun 05, 2010 6:09 pm Looks like it's probably from this. According to this eBay auction which I just happened to stumble upon the other day, it's basically just shots of EoE printed in manga-esque form, like those Tokyopop "cine-manga" you can find of Studio Ghibli movies.

[]: Re: Can someone tell me what I'm looking at here?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jun 05, 2010 6:32 pm Clover wrote: [url]http://evafiles.ifrance.com/html/films/end/end.htm[/url] What art book are these pictures from, if any? Trying to determine if I have in theater shots. (meaningless but cool for me). Guess I'll go for a new Views to Replies ratio record

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sat Jun 05, 2010 8:50 pm -

[]: Re: Can someone tell me what I'm looking at here?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jun 05, 2010 10:43 pm Clover wrote: [url]http://evafiles.ifrance.com/html/films/end/end.htm[/url]Trying to determine if I have in theater shots. (meaningless but cool for me).

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sun Jun 06, 2010 8:32 pm I'm looking for pictures of when Death and EoE were first released into theaters. Promotional materials, pictures from inside a movie theater, film canisters, absolutely anything and everything. Seeing as it was 13 years ago, I doubt anything exists, but I'll keep trying.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jun 06, 2010 8:49 pm I can't think of much like that. There are several theatrical booklets that were released with the movies.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sun Jun 06, 2010 9:14 pm Does anyone still own copies of them? I know that there are scans up, but does anyone have pics of the books themselves?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jun 06, 2010 9:51 pm I have the three program booklets from Death & Rebirth and The End of Evangelion. What are you looking for in photos of the books that scans don't show?

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sun Jun 06, 2010 10:22 pm This is really weird and hard to explain, but... I kinda want to know that they existed... if that makes any sense. Take the red-cross book for instance: sure there are scans and the internet says that they were sold, but how I verify their existence without actually seeing it sitting on a table? It's the same thing for the movies. I know 1.0 and 2.0 existed because of the amount of information available today about them, and because film strips were released in the DVD sets of 2.22, but how do I verify the existence of a 13 year old movie from a foreign country? To me it's just data sitting on my hard drive; scans of DVD sets that supposedly existed, but how do I know? TL;DR - I'm nuts and need to prove to myself that anything exists. Do you have the red cross book? These images will help in my archiving for the greater good of all.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jun 06, 2010 10:59 pm Does this help?

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sun Jun 06, 2010 11:25 pm -

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jun 07, 2010 8:16 am Clover wrote: So, these books were produced and acquired during initial screenings of the films. That is established fact, however the method of purchase remains a mystery
